beats.activity: Wakes up and does a slow morning routine at home, making coffee and laying out a rain-ready day bag.
beats.reasoning: Drizzly weather plus his homebody habits make him start with a calm routine and prep for later.

beats.activity: Quick breakfast and checks emails and a couple of tax-season reminders on his phone.
beats.reasoning: Rain starts early, so he keeps morning light and stays home while he gets mentally organized.

beats.activity: Gathers paperwork and his transit map notebook, then heads out to avoid getting stuck in bridge traffic.
beats.reasoning: He needs to work on Mercer Island, and SR-520 eastbound closure makes an early departure worthwhile.

beats.activity: Stops briefly for coffee and a warm snack before settling into work tasks.
beats.reasoning: Grey, steady rain encourages indoor comfort stops before office focus begins.

beats.activity: Works through tax-season accounting tasks and reconciliations, staying mostly indoors and methodical.
beats.reasoning: As an accountant during tax season, he uses the rainy morning to keep momentum and avoid unnecessary travel.

beats.activity: Picks up dinner to-go and a small grocery top-up for tonight.
beats.reasoning: With rain continuing through midday and his dislike for crowds, he keeps errands efficient and close.

beats.activity: Drives back toward Bothell, taking a slower route to avoid lingering SR-520 traffic.
beats.reasoning: The 9am-noon eastbound closure likely leaves residual congestion; he budgets extra time.

beats.activity: Eats dinner at home, then showers and changes into dry clothes.
beats.reasoning: He gets properly warmed up after a wet commute and keeps the evening cozy.

beats.activity: Does a vinyl check-in: flips through his collection and logs a transit map entry for Mercer Island/nearby routes.
beats.reasoning: He’s easily distracted in a good way—vinyl collecting and transit maps are perfect rainy-day home activities.

beats.activity: Calls a close friend and they trade hiking ideas and campground notes for the next weekend.
beats.reasoning: He values time with close friends more than big crowds, and staying home fits the drizzly weather.

beats.activity: Prepares gear for a future hike, doing a light indoor planning session and setting out essentials.
beats.reasoning: Even if it’s rainy today, he channels hiking energy into planning and small prep at home.

diary:
Woke up in Bothell to that steady, gray drizzly Tuesday—coffee first, always. I packed my rain-ready day bag like I was expecting weather to be stubborn (it was), and laid out the paperwork I needed for tax season. Emails felt extra loud this morning, reminders popping up on my phone while I ate a quick breakfast and tried not to spiral. 

By the time I headed toward Mercer Island, the SR-520 closure had already turned the whole commute into a puzzle. I checked my map notebook, took the safer route, and rolled in around Mercer Island Community Center with my head still mostly intact. After a warm snack at Starbucks, I settled into reconciliation work all day, methodical and quiet. The rain eased off only enough to make it feel like it was thinking about stopping. 

At night I grabbed dinner to-go from QFC, drove back slower than usual, and did a vinyl check-in before calling my friend—hiking plans always help. I felt tired, but good—like I’d handled the day without getting soaked emotionally.
thought_process: I chose to avoid SR-520 early on because I’ve learned that “just wait it out” turns into an hour-long tax-season headache; better to reroute and arrive calm. Keeping my work block indoors at the Mercer Island Community Center was deliberate too—I can reconcile and review numbers best when the world can’t interrupt me with traffic or weather. The drizzly day made everything feel muted, so I leaned into small comforts: a warm snack, a slower drive back, then vinyl and planning. World events (the bridge repair) definitely influenced my schedule, but it didn’t derail my mood; it just nudged my timeline. By evening, once the calls and hiking talk started, my focus shifted from stress to forward motion, and that made me feel lighter.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
